name: Konark Sun Temple
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site known for its chariot-shaped temple dedicated to the Sun God.

detailedInfo.introduction: The Sun Temple at Konark is a 13th-century architectural masterpiece dedicated to the solar deity Surya. Located on the coastline of Odisha, it is renowned for its monumental scale and intricate stone carvings.
detailedInfo.history: Built in the 13th century by King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga dynasty around 1250 CE. It was designed to celebrate military triumphs and honor the Sun God.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: It represents the pinnacle of Kalinga architecture and devotion to Surya. It was once used as a navigational landmark by sailors, who called it the 'Black Pagoda'.
detailedInfo.architecture: The temple is designed as a giant chariot with 24 intricately carved wheels drawn by seven horses. Each wheel acts as a precise sundial.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Open from 6:00 AM to 8:00 PM. Best visited between October and March.
keyFacts: The 24 wheels represent the 24 hours of a day
Famous for its erotic sculptures and celestial dancers

culturalVault.history: The temple was built to commemorate the victory of King Narasimhadeva I over the Muslims. It took 12 years and 12,000 artisans to complete.
culturalVault.stories: Legend says that the temple's peak contained a massive magnet that caused ships to wreck along the shore, leading to its partial dismantling by early sailors.
culturalVault.myths: Local folklore tells of the architect's son, Dharmapada, who completed the temple's crown when the 1,200 masons failed, before jumping into the sea to save his father's reputation.

name: Somnath Temple
description: The first of the twelve holy Jyotirlinga shrines of Lord Shiva, known as the 'Shrine Eternal'.

detailedInfo.introduction: Somnath Temple is a majestic shrine situated on the western coast of Gujarat. It is revered as the first Jyotirlinga of Lord Shiva.
detailedInfo.history: The temple has been destroyed and reconstructed 17 times over centuries. The current structure was rebuilt in 1951.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: It is one of the most sacred pilgrimage sites for Hindus. The name Somnath means 'Lord of the Moon'.
detailedInfo.architecture: Built in the Chalukya style, it features a towering shikhara, intricate carvings, and a stunning view of the Arabian Sea.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Open for Darshan from 6:00 AM to 9:30 PM.
keyFacts: The 'Baan Stambh' indicates a straight line to Antarctica with no land in between

culturalVault.history: The temple's history is a saga of destruction and resurrection.
culturalVault.stories: The moon god, Chandra, is said to have built the original golden temple.
culturalVault.myths: It is believed that the site has been sacred since the Satya Yuga.

name: Kedarnath Temple
description: A sacred Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in the Garhwal Himalayan range.

detailedInfo.introduction: Kedarnath is one of the holiest pilgrim centers in northern India.
detailedInfo.history: Believed to be originally built by the Pandavas and later revived by Adi Shankaracharya.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: It is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas and the most important of the Panch Kedar temples.
detailedInfo.architecture: Constructed with massive grey stone slabs, it is built to survive the harsh Himalayan climate.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Accessible only from April to November.
keyFacts: Survived the 2013 floods due to a massive boulder

culturalVault.history: The Pandavas sought Lord Shiva's penance here after the Kurukshetra war.
culturalVault.stories: Bhairon Baba is believed to protect the temple during winter.
culturalVault.myths: The hump of the bull (Shiva) appeared at Kedarnath.

best_time_to_visit: May to June, September to October

name: Badrinath Temple
description: A holy temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu, part of the main Char Dham pilgrimage.

detailedInfo.introduction: Badrinath Temple is situated along the Alaknanda River in the hill town of Badrinath.
detailedInfo.history: Revived by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: Dedicated to the dual form of Vishnu (Nara-Narayana).
detailedInfo.architecture: Has a colorful facade with a gold-plated roof.
keyFacts: The idol of Lord Vishnu is made of Saligram stone

culturalVault.history: Lord Vishnu performed long penance here shielded by a berry tree.
culturalVault.stories: Mata Murti Ka Mela is a significant festival.
culturalVault.myths: Peak prophecy about the temple vanishing.

best_time_to_visit: May to June, September to October

name: Jagannath Temple
description: A significant Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Jagannath, famous for its annual Rath Yatra.

detailedInfo.introduction: The Jagannath Temple in Puri is one of the holiest Char Dham sites.
detailedInfo.history: Built by King Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva in the 12th century.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: Famous for its mass kitchen and annual chariot festival.
detailedInfo.architecture: Classic Kalinga style architecture with a massive Vimana.
keyFacts: Flag flaps opposite to the wind

culturalVault.history: The wooden idols are renewed every 12 or 19 years.

name: Meenakshi Temple
description: An ancient temple complex in Madurai, a masterpiece of Dravidian architecture.

detailedInfo.introduction: Meenakshi Amman Temple is the heart of the 2,500-year-old city of Madurai.
detailedInfo.history: Rebuilt by the Nayak rulers in the 16th century.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: Dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Sundareswarar.
detailedInfo.architecture: Famous for its 14 towering gopurams and 1000-pillar hall.
detailedInfo.visitingInfo: Open 5:00 AM to 12:30 PM, and 4:00 PM to 9:30 PM.

culturalVault.history: Center of the Tamil Sangam literature.

name: Sanchi Stupa
description: One of the oldest stone structures in India and a significant Buddhist monument.

detailedInfo.introduction: Sanchi Stupa is a UNESCO World Heritage site centered on Buddhist art.
detailedInfo.history: Commissioned by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: Represents the life and journeys of Buddha through symbolic carvings.
detailedInfo.architecture: A hemispherical brick dome with ornate stone gateways.
